perturbation.original_text: This Services Agreement ("Services Agreement" or "Agreement") is entered into as of the date noted below (the "Effective Date") between StartEngine Crowdfunding, Inc., a Delaware corporation ("Company"), and Solutions Vending International, Inc a ___ DE corporation ("Customer" or "you").
perturbation.changed_text: This Services Agreement ("Services Agreement" or "Agreement") is entered into as of the date noted below (the "Effective Date") between StartEngine Crowdfunding, Inc., a Delaware corporation ("Company"), and Solutions Vending International, Inc a ___ DE corporation ("Client" or "you").
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Customer' is changed to 'Client'. While both might seem synonymous, later sections refer specifically to 'Customer' obligations. This change introduces uncertainty as to whether those obligations apply to the 'Client'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Generally In exchange for the Services, you shall pay the Company the then applicable fees and expenses set out below. The Company reserves the right to change the applicable charges and to institute new charges and fees at the end of the Initial Term (as defined below) or then current renewal term, upon 30 days prior notice to you. If you believe that the Company has billed you incorrectly, you must contact Company no later than 60 days after the closing date on the first billing statement in which the error or problem appeared, in order to receive an adjustment or credit. Inquiries should be directed to contact@startengine.com.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Customer' to 'Client' introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text specifically refers to the obligations of the 'Customer'. By renaming 'Customer' to 'Client' in the initial definition, it creates ambiguity as to whether the obligations outlined in the contradicted text apply to 'Client'. This is a clear example of Misaligned Terminology leading to an in-text contradiction, potentially making the enforcement of 'Client' obligations unclear and unenforceable. The contradicted text states 'In exchange for the Services, you shall pay the Company...', and 'you' is now ambiguous because the initial definition now uses 'Client'. Because the definition of terms creates a direct conflict, there is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Customer represents and warrants to the Company that then executed and delivered by Customer, this Service Agreement will constitute the legal, valid, and binding obligation of Customer, enforceable in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.changed_text: Client represents and warrants to the Company that then executed and delivered by Client, this Service Agreement will constitute the legal, valid, and binding obligation of Solutions Vending International, Inc, enforceable in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Customer' to 'Client' in this section and adding company name creates a contradiction because it's unclear if the obligations still apply if the contract refers to 'Customer'. The legal representation is now tied to 'Solutions Vending International, Inc', which makes it unclear if Client and Customer is the same entity in other parts of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_location: 4. Returns and Reversals a) Returns and Reversals
perturbation.contradicted_text: Returns and Reversals User transactions debited from bank accounts via ACH are subject to returns (e.g., non-sufficient funds) and reversals from chargebacks (e.g., unauthorized activity) per the Electronic Fund Transfer Act (15 U.S.C. 1693 et seq. as may be amended), Regulation E, and NACHA guidelines (collectively, such returns and reversals are "Reversals"). The Company will work to protect Customer and the receiving Users from unwarranted Reversals; however, Customer acknowledges and agrees that: i) Customer is liable for all User Activity and Reversals associated with User Activity; ii) If Company's agent receives a Reversal, the Company may in its sole discretion charge Customer the full amount of the Reversal ("Reversed Payment") plus an additional $7 reversal fee ("Reversal Fee" and collectively the "Reversal Liability"); iii) The Company has sole discretion to determine who is at fault and liable for the Reversed Payment and Reversal Fee; iv) Customer authorizes the Company to take any of the following actions (in any particular order): (i) collect the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability from funds sent to your third party escrow account; (ii) debit your bank account in the amount of the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability; (iv) engage in collection efforts to recover the unpaid portion of the Reversal Liability and/or (v) take legal action or any other action under this Service Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change substitutes 'Customer' with 'Client' and then ties the agreement to 'Solutions Vending International, Inc'. The original text defines obligations for 'Customer', but the changed text assigns these obligations to 'Solutions Vending International, Inc'. Since the contradicted text relies on 'Customer' being liable for reversals, this change introduces a contradiction, as it's no longer clear whether 'Customer' or 'Solutions Vending International, Inc' is responsible for the Reversal Liability as stated in the contradicted text. It also leads to ambiguity in other sections of the contract that refers to customer, as defined in 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
